title: quiina guianensis
intro: Quiina guianensis, also known as the South American Quiina or Angelin, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quiina guianensis include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Quiina guianensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its bark can also be used to make a decoction or tincture, showcasing its versatility in herbal preparations.
